nodes.context: Yakuza criminal organization based in Nagoya, Japan; secondary organization of the Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi.
Largest known yakuza syndicate in Japan; the Kodo-kai is a secondary organization of it.
Yamaguchi affiliate; Kodo-kai is the second-largest affiliate after Yamaken-gumi.
Known branch organization of Kodo-kai, based in Hakata, Fukuoka, Kyushu.
Known branch organization of Kodo-kai, based in Tokyo.
Known branch organization of Kodo-kai, based in Chiba.
Known branch organization of Kodo-kai, based in Sapporo, Hokkaido.
Known branch organization of Kodo-kai, based in Nagano; largest Kodo affiliate in Nagano Prefecture.
Known branch organization of Kodo-kai, based in Otsu, Shiga; founded in 2003.

edges.evidence_quote: It is a secondary organization of the Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Kodo-kai is a subordinate group within the Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: it is the second-largest Yamaguchi affiliate after the Yamaken-gumi
edges.context: Kodo-kai is compared as the second-largest affiliate after Yamaken-gumi, indicating a structural relationship within the same syndicate.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Seibu Rengo, Komatsu-gumi, Koike-gumi, Fukushima Rengo, Jido-kai, Omi-ikka
edges.context: Seibu Rengo is listed as an ally of Kodo-kai in the infobox.
